last_digest.green_note: 3 handled well: fresh all-brands CRM/WorkOS outage (14303) escalated and communicated fast despite being P1; fresh Star Casino NL bonus-distributor alert (14301) has a lead, too fresh to flag; Data Science message-spike incident (14284) unchanged since this morning, still fresh.

synthesis.summary: CRM login unavailable across all brands due to a WorkOS (third-party auth provider) outage. Joachim acknowledged and started investigating within seconds; root cause (WorkOS-side, confirmed via their status page and the authorize-URL timeout) identified within ~1 minute. Patrik joined immediately; an Intercom banner comms went out to affected users ~18 minutes after the alert. As of this check the outage is only partially recovered (a few brands not on WorkOS, e.g. Leonbets, are unaffected; others still very slow).
key_events: 16 Jul 08:13:03 CEST alert fires, Joachim acknowledges instantly
08:14 CEST Joachim identifies the failing WorkOS authorize URL and timeout
08:15-08:16 CEST Patrik confirms WorkOS-side (their status page), team floods in
08:17 CEST comms plan agreed: Intercom banner to logged-in and logged-out users
08:31 CEST banner live
08:30-08:35 CEST mixed reports: some brands (not on WorkOS, e.g. Leonbets) unaffected, others still very slow/failing
proposals: Wait on WorkOS to recover (external dependency) - in progress
Intercom banner comms to affected users - done, ~18min after alert
synthesis.why: Fast, correct handling of a fresh all-brands P1: instant acknowledgment, root cause found in ~1 minute, CTO engaged immediately, partner-facing comms out within 18 minutes (well under the C9 threshold) - too fresh and too well-handled to flag. Watch for full recovery confirmation and a closing update.

synthesis.summary: MeridianBet couldn't add sports bonuses in a Peru onboarding flow. Matteo matched it to a prior incident and got an existing FE fix deployed, asking the partner to retest - but the partner reported the fix still failed, and a second user reported a related failure on a different action. Hours later, Hikmet found a genuinely different root cause (a hidden required AuthType field with no default) and fixed it directly. Joachim followed through on the morning plan and asked the partner via Intercom to re-check; awaiting their reply.

synthesis.summary: Significant progress since last check. Samuela root-caused why ~120 of the ~3,545 brand-wide mismatched game mappings survived the reconciliation endpoint: three resolver failure modes. Two ('&'-naming and non-ASCII title stripping) are root-caused, fixed, tested, and deploying tomorrow AM. The third (alias-shadowed games) is understood but not yet fixed - needs a quick owner decision, doesn't currently affect active challenges. The specific customer-impacting cases were manually corrected in the DB so player challenge-credit is no longer blocked. incident.io was substantively updated (17:02 UTC) with the full narrative.

INC-10938.name: Security incident: Malicious dashboard creation attempt

synthesis.summary: Recurring H2Bet nightly ClickHouse-writer queue pileup, consolidated into this 'DO NOT CLOSE' bucket since 26 Jun. A separate log-volume config bug was fixed, but the producer/consumer payload type-mismatch itself is still unfixed. The promised 'close Monday 13 Jul if stable' checkpoint has now passed by 3 days with no update logged, and there was no new Slack activity this cycle.
key_events: No new Slack messages
incident.io updated_at ticked to 14 Jul 13:29 but visible status-update text unchanged, still dated 09 Jul
proposals: Schema/type-mismatch fix - still not implemented, only the reload-loop/log-volume side-fix has landed
open_breaches: A4: root-cause payload type-mismatch bug identified 26 Jun, still unfixed ~20 days later, with 2 more recurrences merged in as late as 12 Jul - a recurring issue with no resolution plan
D15: incident.io stale - no substantive update since 09 Jul, the promised 13 Jul close-if-stable checkpoint has now passed by 3 days unaddressed
synthesis.why: This matches the 'recurring issue heading for silent close' pattern the guidelines call out directly - a P4 bucket incident can persist indefinitely under 'DO NOT CLOSE', and the actual schema fix still has no owner or date attached.
synthesis.suggested_action: Simon Brown to either commit to a concrete date for the producer/consumer schema fix (not just further monitoring) or formally acknowledge the missed 13 Jul checkpoint with a revised one.

INC-10902.name: H2Bet incidents - night - DO NOT CLOSE

synthesis.summary: 12play/we88 (Momentum) players received rewards 45-50min late due to RDS overload, a known daily 4pm choke pattern that finally got a real fix (4 PRs, 2 DB upgrades over 3 weeks). Daniel Lindengren's 15 Jul handover to Sam Lundborg (RabbitMQ-upgrade coordination/cutover) was properly executed in Slack, but incident.io still lists Daniel (on leave until 10 Aug) as Incident Lead and status is unchanged at 'Fixing' since 10 Jul. No new activity this cycle beyond what was already captured.
key_events: No new messages this cycle; incident.io roles/status unchanged

promises: update 12play via Intercom on fix progress - discussed internally, completion not confirmed

proposals: RDS/cluster upgrade maintenance window scheduled for next Wednesday (~1am FT time) with the partner
RabbitMQ 4.x cutover coordination now owned by Sam Lundborg
open_breaches: D15: incident.io status stuck 'Fixing' since 10 Jul and Incident Lead field still shows Daniel Lindengren (on leave until 10 Aug) - the real handover happened in Slack but hasn't been reflected in the record
synthesis.why: The substance is well in hand (fix landed, upgrade window scheduled with the partner) - purely a record-hygiene gap, but leaving someone on a month's leave listed as Incident Lead on an active P3 is a real handover risk.
synthesis.suggested_action: Sam Lundborg to formally reassign Incident Lead to himself, referencing the 15 Jul Slack handover, ahead of the scheduled maintenance window next Wednesday.

Partner (Anna Koller) agreed to a weekly manual re-sync as interim mitigation, confirmed GiG never shipped a fix on their end. No new activity since last check. follow_up_list confirms zero follow-ups exist, so the update_epoch fix remains untracked despite the partner actively waiting on it.

synthesis.why: Partner engagement remains good and stable, but the concrete long-term fix commitment still has no tracked owner or due date - same gap as last run, now directly confirmed via follow_up_list.
synthesis.suggested_action: Create a tracked incident.io follow-up for the update_epoch ordering-key fix with an owner and due date - still outstanding.

synthesis.summary: Fleet-wide RDS Performance Schema memory leak, fixed via PR #416, rolled out fleet-wide by 9 Jul. On 14 Jul the lead closed remaining gaps: the capping follow-up is completed, and a new RAM/swap-alarm follow-up was created and assigned. follow_up_list confirms only the activity-manager-go resilience follow-up remains outstanding and still unassigned.
key_events: follow_up_list(INC-9820) confirms: capping follow-up completed; RAM/swap alarm follow-up outstanding, assigned to Simon Brown/SRE; activity-manager-go resilience follow-up outstanding, unassigned
proposals: Completed: cap performance_schema memory (PR #416)
In progress: RDS/Aurora memory & swap alarm thresholds (assigned)
Outstanding, unassigned: bounded retry/backoff for activity-manager-go on transient MySQL OOM errors

synthesis.why: Steady, no regressions; only the resilience-hardening follow-up still lacks an owner as the incident heads toward closure.
synthesis.suggested_action: Assign an owner to the activity-manager-go resilience follow-up before this incident is closed out.

INC-9820.name: RDS OOM all brands - gradually increasing overtime

synthesis.summary: H2Bet duplicate bonus grants confirmed root cause quickly, but 50+ days later still ~300 duplicate grants/week with no fix deployed. BetConstruct account-manager gap means zero external traction since 5 Jun. Rewards team's own Urgent ticket (REW-176) remains blocked by capacity until mid-Aug. Re-checked: 0 new Slack messages, 0 new Intercom parts (conversation still closed since 06 Jul), incident.io metadata completely unchanged - situation fully static, financial leakage continuing unabated.
key_events: 26 May incident opened, root cause confirmed same day
29 May H2Bet rejects proposed 503-as-success workaround
18 Jun root-cause refresh confirms no technical mitigation deployed, PR #122 closed unmerged
since 18 Jun no further incident.io updates
15/16 Jul re-checks: 0 new Slack messages, 0 new Intercom parts, Intercom conversation still shows closed while incident.io stays open P1 - no movement whatsoever
proposals: 503-as-success workaround (May) - rejected by partner 28 May
PR #122 idempotency fix - closed unmerged
PR #123 observability only - merged, no fix
REW-176 interim workaround - ACCEPTED by H2Bet (per management note 16 Jul), opened Urgent 9 Jul, blocked by Rewards capacity until mid-Aug
open_breaches: A2/A4: no decisive fix after 50+ days, Urgent ticket parked a month+
A3: minimizing language contradicts confirmed weekly leakage
B5: BetConstruct escalation zero traction
C10/D15: Intercom closed (since 06 Jul) implying resolution while incident.io still open P1
synthesis.why: ~300 duplicate bonus grants/week continuing after 50+ days; the accepted interim workaround (REW-176, per management note 16 Jul) is parked until mid-Aug by Rewards capacity, and the BetConstruct root-cause path has zero traction (A2/A4/B5). The open decision is scheduling/risk, not partner sign-off.
synthesis.suggested_action: Decide this week: pull REW-176 forward in Rewards' queue, or formally document risk-acceptance of the leakage until mid-Aug; separately escalate the BetConstruct account-manager gap to leadership.

management_notes.note: Andrea: correction — H2Bet ACCEPTED the interim workaround (discussed in the meeting with them and our Partner Manager, early Jul); that acceptance is why REW-176 was opened on 9 Jul. The 28 May rejection was the earlier 503-as-success proposal only. No sign-off is pending from H2Bet.

synthesis.summary: SMS distributor service has not processed queued messages for all Coinbar brands since before 30 Apr - over 1M messages backlogged. Root cause identified almost immediately, but 2.5+ months later there is zero forward progress. Re-checked: 0 new Slack messages, incident.io metadata fully unchanged - confirmed nothing has moved since 29 May besides automated alert noise.
key_events: 30 Apr incident opened, 5 brand queues found with zero consumers, 1M+ messages backlogged
external SMS provider outage identified as root cause almost immediately
29 May one substantive question raised in-channel - never answered or tracked
since 29 May pure automated churn, lead rotated to at least a 6th person
15/16 Jul re-checks: 0 new Slack messages, incident.io unchanged - still zero decisive action
open_breaches: Awaiting partner feedback (credit issue) — keep a periodic chase cadence so the wait stays deliberate
synthesis.why: Downgraded to 🟡 per management note 16 Jul: the SMS delivery failure is a known credit-related issue with the partner; they are aware and FT is awaiting their feedback — blocked on the partner, not on FT inaction. Watch: the block is long-running (~2.5 months), so periodic chasing keeps it from drifting into a permanent dead letter.
synthesis.suggested_action: Set a chase rhythm with the partner (e.g. weekly) and record each nudge in the incident; if their credit situation won't resolve, decide deliberately whether to purge/park the backlog and close.

management_notes.note: Andrea: known issue with the partner relating to credit — the partner is aware and we are awaiting their further feedback. Watch-level, not escalate.
